header: how can i get cachy's kernel and drivers?
paragraph: first grab the kernel: sudo pacman -S linux-cachyos

if you have nvidia, u can use the normal dkms drivers that was optional in the installation or if you are going to *only* use the cachy kernel, u can use the linux-cachyos-nvidia-open driver and get rid of dkms
if you want to get rid of dkms and only use the cachy kernel, do: sudo pacman -S linux-cachyos-nvidia-open
to get rid of the regular kernel run this: sudo pacman -R linux
but i *wouldn't* recommend getting rid of the regular kernel so if you want to keep the regular kernel and use nvidia too, you can run: sudo pacman -S nvidia-open
you only need to set the locals its not like archinstall

header: which type of users is kyonlinux for?
paragraph: kyon linux is for elementary (not the os) linux users such as cachyOS users that know how to use pacman and other things
kyon linux is also for advanced users that want their distro preconfigured to save time, etc. kyonlinux also has some repos such as:
chaotic-aur (for people who want binary packages from aur)
for example, elementary (not the os) users can go and use cachy's optimised kernel and its drivers in kyonlinux
and advanced users can use the nix package manager
but i heavily recommend this distro to elementary (NOT THE OS) users!
